metadata.title: Build a Wave Packet Spreading Sim with Claude Code

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Prompt Claude Code with the formula, constants, and exactly what to draw for a Gaussian packet. Read the generated code and verify tau equals two m sigma-zero squared over h-bar, about 17.3 femtoseconds. Run it and check the sigma curve reads 1.414 nanometers at tau and the Gaussian is visibly broader. Iterate with one small diff and one re-run; the loop, not the packet, is the takeaway.

artifactLines: Prompt Claude Code with the formula, constants, and exactly what to draw for a Gaussian packet.
Read the generated code and verify tau equals two m sigma-zero squared over h-bar, about 17.3 femtoseconds.
Run it and check the sigma curve reads 1.414 nanometers at tau and the Gaussian is visibly broader.
Iterate with one small diff and one re-run; the loop, not the packet, is the takeaway.

beats.narration_text: I love that the packet was just the vehicle and the prompt-read-run-check-change loop was the point. I want to run that loop myself. Please: (1) write the exact Claude Code prompt I'd type to generate the Manim scene for sigma-zero equals 2 nanometers instead of 1, (2) tell me the two numbers I should hand-check before rendering, tau and sigma at tau, with their new values, and (3) give me the one-line iterate diff to add a dashed marker at the new doubling time.

props.segment: Build a Wave Packet Spreading Sim with Claude Code
props.command: I love that the packet was just the vehicle and the prompt-read-run-check-change loop was the point. I want to run that loop myself. Please: (1) write the exact Claude Code prompt I'd type to generate the Manim scene for sigma-zero equals 2 nanometers instead of 1, (2) tell me the two numbers I should hand-check before rendering, tau and sigma at tau, with their new values, and (3) give me the one-line iterate diff to add a dashed marker at the new doubling time.

beats.narration_text: The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, Gaussian wave packet in free space, sigma-zero equals one nanometer, electron mass. Plot sigma of t versus t in femtoseconds. Show the packet width reaching square-root-two times sigma-zero at the doubling time tau. Label the doubling time on the curve. Specify: the formula, the constants, and what to draw.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  Gaussian wave packet, free space
  σ₀ = 1 nm,  m = mₑ
  plot σ(t) vs t in fs
  label doubling time τ = 2mσ₀²/ℏ ≈ 17.3 fs
  show σ(τ) = √2·σ₀"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes it. Read before running. Find this line: sigma-zero times square-root of one plus open-paren h-bar t over two m sigma-zero squared close-paren squared. Two numbers to check: tau equals two m sigma-zero squared over h-bar — should be seventeen-point-three femtoseconds. And sigma at tau equals square-root-two times sigma-zero — exactly one-point-four-one-four nanometers. Thirty seconds. Catch it before the render.

props.code:
# packet.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np
hbar  = 1.0546e-34   # J·s
m_e   = 9.109e-31    # kg
sigma0 = 1e-9        # 1 nm

tau = 2*m_e*sigma0**2/hbar  # 17.3 fs

def sigma(t):                # ← the formula
    return sigma0*np.sqrt(1+(hbar*t/(2*m_e*sigma0**2))**2)

# sigma(0)   = 1.000 nm
# sigma(tau) = 1.414 nm  = √2·σ₀  ✓

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two checks. First: the sigma curve at t equals tau. The marker reads one-point-four-one-four nanometers — square-root-two times one — exactly. Second: the Gaussian at t equals tau. It is visibly broader than at t equals zero, same area, lower peak. Both correct. The packet spreads not because quantum mechanics is uncertain about where it is — but because different momentum components travel at different speeds.

Iterate: claude — update packet dot py — add a vertical dashed line at tau on the sigma curve, label it tau equals seventeen-point-three femtoseconds. One diff, one re-run. Prompt, read, run, check, change. The packet was the vehicle. The loop is the takeaway.

props.command:
claude "update packet.py:
  → add dashed vertical at t=τ on σ(t) plot
  → label τ = 17.3 fs"

beats.narration_text: The packet is spreading right now. Paste this into Claude and put a barrier in its path.

props.segment: Build a Wave Packet Spreading Sim with Claude Code
props.command: claude "extend the wave packet simulation: add a barrier potential, show partial transmission and reflection, animate the tunneling probability vs barrier width"
